title: Balloon containment device
abstract: The present invention provides a balloon containment device comprising a central portion having a diameter larger than the diameter of the narrowest part of an expanded opening portion of a balloon. An elongate external retainer extends from the central portion toward a proximate end to the balloon opening portion and having a length enabled for placement of the central portion, extending through a neck portion of the balloon opening portion. An elongate internal retainer extends from the central portion, and toward a distal end from the balloon opening portion, and having a length designed for placement inside of the balloon. Wherein when the balloon is inflated, the central portion is held against the neck portion by manipulation of the external retainer.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A balloon containment device comprising: a central portion having a diameter larger than the diameter of the narrowest part of an expanded opening portion of a balloon; an elongate external retainer extending from the central portion toward a proximate end to the balloon opening portion and having a length enabled for placement of the central portion, extending through a neck portion of the balloon opening portion; and an elongate internal retainer extending from the central portion, and toward a distal end from the balloon opening portion, and having a length designed for placement inside of the balloon; wherein when the balloon is inflated, the central portion is held against the neck portion by manipulation of the external retainer. 2. The balloon containment device of claim 1, wherein the external retainer end and the internal retainer end each have the same shape. 3. The balloon containment device of claim 1, wherein the internal retainer is formed in a circular shape. 4. The balloon containment device of claim 1, wherein the internal retainer is formed in a triangular shape. 5. The balloon containment device of claim 1, wherein the balloon opening portion is tubular and the elongate external retainer extends from the central portion and includes at least one hook that positions the external connector to a rim at the tubular end of the balloon. 6. The balloon containment device of claim 1, wherein the external retainer is formed in a cross shape. 7. The balloon containment device of claim 1, wherein the external retainer extends through a wall of the neck portion of the balloon opening portion. 8. The balloon containment device of claim 1, wherein the external retainer is curved. 9. A method of maintaining a volume in a balloon while inflating, comprising; (a) providing a deflated balloon; (b) inserting through an opening portion of the balloon, an elongate internal retainer extending from a central portion of a balloon containment device; (c) maintaining at a proximate end to the opening portion of the balloon an elongate external retainer partially extending outside of the balloon; (d) inflating, or partially inflating the balloon and manipulating the elongate internal retainer and elongate external container to maintain a volume of inflation within the balloon. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the external retainer partially extends through a neck of the opening portion of the balloon. 11. The method of claim 9, wherein the external retainer end and the internal retainer end each have the same shape. 12. The method of claim 9, wherein the internal retainer is formed in a circular shape. 13. The method of claim 9, wherein the internal retainer is formed in a triangular shape. 14. The method of claim 9, wherein the balloon opening portion is tubular and the elongate external connector extends from the central portion and includes at least one hook that positions the external connector to a rim at the tubular end of the balloon.
